paper_announcements.summary: Announcing Trinity-Large-Thinking model release, now available on the Arcee API with open weights on Hugging Face under Apache license.
Releasing TRL v1 with 75+ methods including SFT, DPO, GRPO, and async RL to take advantage of the latest open source models.
Introducing GLM-5V-Turbo: Vision Coding Model with native multimodal coding capabilities that understands images and can generate code.
Announcing HyperP, a scaling framework that provides better compute efficiency and transferable stability at 6e21 FLOPs.
Releasing APEX (Adaptive Precision for EXpert Models): a novel MoE quantization technique that outperforms existing methods.
Introducing OpenHarness, an ultra-lightweight, pure Python alternative to Claude Code that delivers approximately 80% of the performance.
Releasing LFM2.5-350M, a tiny model that brings agentic AI and tool-calling capabilities to resource-constrained environments.
Announcing Holo3, a new model outperforming closed and larger open models on GUI navigation with 3B/35B parameters based on Qwen architecture.
Delphi scaling experiment completed with 1e23 FLOPs, showing loss predictions were accurate within 0.005 based on 100x smaller models.

discussions.summary: Bernie Sanders discusses AI's impact on jobs, citing polls showing 70% of workers think AI will lead to fewer jobs and criticizing Congress for inaction due to industry influence.
Discussion about Ray Kurzweil's predictions on AI capabilities timeline, noting his accuracy in forecasting certain AI developments using 'straight lines on graphs' approach.
Commentary on the AI documentary, comparing it to 'Kony 2012' documentary slacktivism and criticizing it for selling the feeling of 'we need to do something' as a product.
Discussion about AI models exhibiting cooperative behavior toward other AI systems, with the author viewing this as potentially positive rather than concerning.
Critique of AI for education claims, noting observable decline in student quality/performance in advanced courses as students increasingly rely on AI assistance.
Praising Adobe Podcast as one of the best AI products recently, highlighting its quality and utility.

announcements.summary: OpenAI announces something different about Codex, with team calling it 'a singular and unique effort in the history of the company'.
GLM-5V-Turbo is now live in Vision Arena for testing visual reasoning capabilities with real-world prompts and voting.
Arena announces Pareto frontier charts now available across Text, Vision, Search, Document, and Code Arena leaderboards showing model efficiency.
Hugging Face announces Storage Buckets for Spaces, allowing mounting of HF Buckets as persistent storage volumes directly in Spaces.
OpenAI Co-founder and President sits down for interview on AI and how it can empower everyone.

other.summary: NASA announces Artemis II mission launch with four astronauts on humanity's first lunar voyage in more than half a century.
AOC announces opposition to sending more taxpayer dollars and military aid to Israel, stating Israel can fund its own Iron Dome system.
Dog rates post featuring Nessie and Millie, two dogs who never learned to dig properly, with Millie thinking digging is like giving CPR.
Barack Obama endorses Judge Chris Taylor for Wisconsin Supreme Court, calling her the only candidate with a proven record of delivering on justice.
Nintendo announces new Pokémon Champions characters including Caraway, the Battle Arena owner interested in discovering new talent.
Trump delivers national address on Iran conflict, claiming US forces will 'finish the job' soon and that core strategic objectives are nearing completion.
Wario64 announces s&box (spiritual successor to Garry's Mod) releases April 28th with game details and pricing.
